Other Teaching Activities

Summary of Teaching Experience:


Professor at Simon Fraser University
Courses: Canadian Political Economy (second year), Public Administration (second year), Public Policy Process (third year), Governing Instruments (third year), Public Administration (Graduate)

Associate Professor at Simon Fraser University
Courses: Democracy and the Policy Process (third year), Governing Instruments (third year), Public Policy Analysis (fourth year), Public Policy Process (third year), Canadian Political Economy (second year), Canadian Natural Resource and Environmental Policy (fourth year), Canadian Public Policy (Graduate), Issues in Social and Economic Policy (Graduate)

Assistant Professor at Simon Fraser University
Courses: Public Policy Analysis (fourth year), Canadian Politics (second year), Canadian Political Economy (second year), Introduction to Political Science (first year), Provincial Politics (third year), Canadian Public Policy (Graduate)

Assistant Professor (limited term) at Simon Fraser University
Courses: Introduction to Political Sciences (first year), Canadian Government (second year), Canadian Public Administration (second year), Canadian Political Economy (second year), Canadian Provincial Politics (third year), Natural Resource and Environmental Policy (fourth year), Public Policy Analysis (fourth year)

Visiting Lecturer at University of Victoria
Courses: Canadian Politics (first year), Public Administration (third year), Public Services (third year), Canadian Natural Resources and Environmental Policy (fourth year)

Adjunct Lecturer at Queen's University
Courses: Public Administration (third year), Canadian Natural Resource Policy (fourth year)

Current Research Interests

Analyizing Public Policy Capacity in Canada
� �
Administrative Styles in Canada: Sub and Cross-National Variations and Contexts [public administration, Canada, provincial politics, public policy, comparative politics]
� �
Policy Analysis in Canada [public management, governance, Canada, public policy, public administration]
� �
Time and Policy Analysis: Path Dependency, Process Tracing and Narratology in the Policy Sciences
� �
Mapping Policy Networks: An Empirical Evaluation of Policy Subsystems in Canada [Canada, Public Policy, Policy Networks, Policy Subsystems]
� �
Multi-Level, Multi-Round, Multi-Arena Decision-Making: Evidence and Experience in Canada [public policy, decision-making]
� �
Post-Staples Political Economy in Canada
� �
